behavior_summary: When the player examines the obsidian coins item (U8P_COINS::look), the script reads the stack quantity, formats a short human-readable string like '<n> obsidian coin' or '<n> obsidian coins' with correct pluralization, displays that string to the player as a Bark, then suspends/ends the U8P_COINS::look process.

A helper process U8P_COINS::combine adjusts the item's visual frame to reflect stack size: if quantity < 6 it sets the frame to quantity-1, otherwise it clamps the frame to 5. This changes how stacked coins appear in the UI/world.

The script operates on the implicit item instance ('this') and relies on engine/item APIs (Item::getQuantity, Item::setFrame) plus utility functions for string conversion and output (numToStr, Bark, suspend).
notable_control_flow: Pluralization branch in U8P_COINS::look: append 's' to 'coin' when the quantity is not exactly 1.
U8P_COINS::look performs: read quantity -> convert number to string -> build message -> Bark -> suspend -> clear temporary string.
U8P_COINS::combine performs: read quantity -> if quantity < 6 set frame = quantity - 1 else set frame = 5 (clamping behavior to a max visual index).
open_questions: Is U8P_COINS::combine invoked automatically by inventory/U8P_COINS::combine actions or only manually from other code paths? (not present in this extract)
Does the visual frame index mapping (0..5) correspond exactly to stacked-coin sprites for quantities 1..6+ in all UI contexts?
Are there any external systems (e.g., stack-splitting or merge handlers) that call U8P_COINS::combine when quantity changes, or must that be invoked explicitly by gameplay code?

main_function.name: U8P_COINS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short description when the player examines the obsidian coins item: report the count with correct pluralization and display it as a short on-screen bark.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player 'looks at' / examines the coins item (U8P_COINS::look/examine event for this item instance).

function_map.name: U8P_COINS::look
function_map.role: Examine handler that builds and displays a coin-count string to the player.

function_map.name: U8P_COINS::combine
function_map.role: Utility/process that sets the item's visual frame based on stack quantity (visual update for stacked coins).

function_map.name: Item::getQuantity
function_map.role: Engine/item API to read the numeric quantity of this item stack.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Engine/item API to set visual frame/index for the item (used to reflect stack size).

function_map.name: numToStr
function_map.role: Utility to convert numeric quantity to a display string.

function_map.name: Bark
function_map.role: UI/output API used to present a short message to the player.

function_map.name: suspend
function_map.role: Process control primitive used to yield/terminate the U8P_COINS::look process after showing the bark.

external_calls.purpose: Read the current stack quantity of this item instance to display to the player.
Convert the numeric quantity into a string fragment for the display message.
Show the constructed '<n> obsidian coin(s)' message to the player.
Yield or terminate the U8P_COINS::look process after the bark has been displayed.
Obtain the stack quantity to decide which visual frame to set.
Set the item instance's visual frame to reflect stack size (frame = quantity-1 for qty<6, else frame=5).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the player examines the obsidian coins item (U8P_COINS::look), the script reads the stack quantity, formats a short human-readable string like '<n> obsidian coin' or '<n> obsidian coins' with correct pluralization, displays that string to the player as a Bark, then suspends/ends the U8P_COINS::look process.
